content: Just as the human learning framework in high school language arts frames 'reflexion' as a deliberate vocabulary and critical thinking exercise that builds enduring conceptual understanding over repeated practice, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to follow this scaffolded educational structure, rather than relying on ad-hoc single-step self-critique. This approach would treat LLM self-reflection as a graded, repeated skill-building exercise similar to how students learn to reflect on their own writing to improve over time.
